metadata.title: Token Standards and Crypto Economics Knowledge Graph
metadata.description: Concept dependency graph for Token Standards & Crypto Valuation — 18 nodes, 22 edges
metadata.source: ERC-20/721 Standards, Chris Dixon 'Crypto Tokens' (2017), a16z 'Tokenology' (2023), Metcalfe's Law & Crypto Valuation

nodes.name: Fungible Tokens: The Concept
nodes.description: Understanding fungibility: tokens where each unit is identical and interchangeable. Like currency, each token has the same value as any other token of the same type.
key_ideas: Fungibility means each token is identical in type and value
Like ETH or traditional currency, 1 token = 1 token always
Foundation for ERC-20 and most cryptocurrency tokens

nodes.name: ERC-20 Token Standard
nodes.description: The standard API for fungible tokens on Ethereum. Defines methods like transfer, balanceOf, approve, and events like Transfer and Approval.
key_ideas: Proposed by Fabian Vogelsteller in November 2015
Implements 9 methods: name, symbol, decimals, totalSupply, balanceOf, transfer, transferFrom, approve, allowance
Two events: Transfer, Approval
Enables interoperability: wallets, exchanges, DApps can work with any ERC-20 token

nodes.name: ERC-20 Token Loss Problem
nodes.description: A critical design flaw: tokens sent to contracts without ERC-20 handling are permanently locked. Over $83M lost as of 2024.
key_ideas: Contracts cannot detect incoming ERC-20 transfers (no callback)
Tokens sent to unprepared contracts are permanently locked
Minimum $83.6M USD lost as of June 2024
Solutions: require(_to != address(this)), approve+transferFrom pattern, ERC-223/ERC-1363

nodes.name: Non-Fungible Tokens (NFTs): The Concept
nodes.description: Understanding non-fungibility: each token is unique and can have different value. Like a concert ticket with a specific seat number.
key_ideas: Non-fungible means each token is unique
Each NFT has a unique tokenId (uint256)
Perfect for collectibles, access keys, tickets, digital art

nodes.name: ERC-721 NFT Standard
nodes.description: The standard API for non-fungible tokens on Ethereum. Each token has a unique tokenId and the pair (contract address, tokenId) is globally unique.
key_ideas: Proposed by William Entriken, Dieter Shirley, Jacob Evans, Nastassia Sachs in January 2018
Core methods: balanceOf, ownerOf, safeTransferFrom, transferFrom, approve, setApprovalForAll
Events: Transfer, Approval, ApprovalForAll
Global uniqueness: (contract address, uint256 tokenId) pair

nodes.name: CryptoKitties: NFT Case Study
nodes.description: The 2017 NFT game that congested Ethereum and popularized NFTs. A historical milestone showing NFT's potential and limitations.
key_ideas: Launched in 2017, became so popular it congested Ethereum network
First mainstream demonstration of NFT concept
Custom events: Pregnant, Birth
2026 context: NFT market crashed 93%+ from 2021 peak, but technology evolved to utility use cases

nodes.name: The Centralization of the Internet
nodes.description: How the open internet was captured by Big Tech (Google, Facebook, Amazon, Apple) through proprietary platforms and 30% platform taxes.
key_ideas: Internet started as open protocols, anyone could build on equal footing
Google started in a garage, Facebook in a dorm room
By 2017, Big Tech dominated market cap, mobile apps, developer platforms
iOS/Android charge 30% fees and control app distribution
nodes.paper_ref: Chris Dixon — Crypto Tokens: A Breakthrough (2017)

nodes.name: Bitcoin's Dual Innovation
nodes.description: Bitcoin contained two innovations: (1) alternative financial system, (2) new way to build open networks. Tokens extracted and generalized the second.
key_ideas: Innovation 1: Store of value, alternative to traditional finance
Innovation 2: Open network design primitive (tokens)
Crypto tokens separated innovation #2 from Bitcoin's financial context
nodes.paper_ref: Chris Dixon — Crypto Tokens: A Breakthrough (2017)

nodes.name: Ethereum and Smart Contracts
nodes.description: Ethereum as the first major non-Bitcoin token network. Introduced smart contracts and the Ether token for computation ('gas').
key_ideas: Whitepaper 2013, launch 2015 (Dixon incorrectly wrote 2014 introduction)
Vitalik wanted smart contracts on Bitcoin, limitations led to new approach
Smart contracts: code executed by distributed network
Ether token: financial asset + purchase computation (gas)
nodes.paper_ref: Chris Dixon — Crypto Tokens: A Breakthrough (2017)

nodes.name: Token-Enabled Open Service Governance
nodes.description: How tokens solve the open network's biggest weakness: inability to manage and finance running services at scale.
key_ideas: Open systems lacked effective ways to manage and fund operational services
2007 social network wars: open protocols lost to proprietary services (Facebook)
Tokens fund hosting costs via automatic distribution to miners/validators
Tokens create shared computing resources while maintaining decentralization
nodes.paper_ref: Chris Dixon — Crypto Tokens: A Breakthrough (2017)

nodes.name: Incentive Alignment Through Tokens
nodes.description: How tokens align all network participants (users, developers, investors, service providers) toward common goal: network growth and token value appreciation.
key_ideas: Traditional platforms: conflict between platform and complements (Twitter API shutdowns)
Token networks: everyone benefits from network growth
Solves bootstrap problem: early incentives attract initial users
Steemit example: paid users for content, token value rose, virtuous cycle (2017 optimism; 2026 reality: fork to Hive after governance failure)
nodes.paper_ref: Chris Dixon — Crypto Tokens: A Breakthrough (2017)

nodes.name: Token Distribution Design
nodes.description: Careful management of token allocation across five groups: users, core devs, 3rd party devs, investors, service providers to maximize network growth.
key_ideas: Five participant groups must receive appropriate token allocation
Balance determines network growth trajectory
Analogy: imagine if internet was funded by domain name sales, with names distributed to builders, hosters, developers
nodes.paper_ref: Chris Dixon — Crypto Tokens: A Breakthrough (2017)

nodes.name: The Open Network Movement
nodes.description: Crypto as spiritual successor to open source (Linux) and open information (Wikipedia) movements. Combines social benefits of open protocols with financial advantages of proprietary networks.
key_ideas: Linux: open source software → now dominant OS
Wikipedia: open information → most popular reference
Both were niche and controversial at first, late blooming (大器晚成)
Crypto tokens: open network design, currently niche but potentially transformative
nodes.paper_ref: Chris Dixon — Crypto Tokens: A Breakthrough (2017)

nodes.name: Tokens as New Digital Primitive
nodes.description: Chris Dixon's 2021 insight: tokens are to Web3 what websites were to Web 1.0 — a fundamental building block.
key_ideas: 1990s: website = new digital primitive
2020s: token = new digital primitive
Enables new forms of value creation and organization

nodes.name: Tokenology: Beyond Tokenomics
nodes.description: Guy Wuollet's framework: tokens are vectors (multidimensional) not scalars. They express social, reputational, and political value beyond monetary.
key_ideas: Tokenomics is too narrow, focuses only on economic dimension
Tokenology = tokens × economics × art × cryptography × mechanism design
Tokens are vectors: magnitude + direction, multidimensional value
Modern economy reduces high-dimensional value to scalar (USD), losing information

nodes.name: Four Token Use Cases
nodes.description: Ownership, Alignment, Incentive structures, Access to goods/services — the four primary ways tokens create value beyond money.
key_ideas: Ownership: User-owned networks (Ethereum), hyperstructures
Alignment: Creator coins, social tokens, community membership (BAYC 2023; floor -90% by 2026)
Incentive: Governance (Uniswap, Compound), validator coordination
Access: Compute (Ethereum gas), storage (Filecoin), token-gated experiences

nodes.name: Metcalfe's Law for Networks
nodes.description: Network value is proportional to the square of users (n²). Originally for Ethernet, applied to Facebook revenue and crypto valuation.
key_ideas: Robert Metcalfe (1980s): network value ∝ n²
2015: Facebook revenue ∝ MAU², also Tencent
Ken Alabi: Bitcoin, Ethereum, Dash fit Metcalfe's Law
Foundation for crypto valuation models
nodes.paper_ref: Jacob Franek — Metcalfe's Law & Crypto Valuation (2018)

nodes.name: NVT Ratio: Crypto Valuation Metric
nodes.description: Network Value-to-Transactions ratio. When NVT > 20, price may exceed fundamental value. Similar to P/E ratio for stocks.
key_ideas: NVT = Market Cap / 90-day MA of daily USD transaction volume
NVT > 20 suggests overvaluation (Kalichkin)
Limitations: both numerator and denominator contain USD price, circular dependency
Successfully predicted major corrections in BTC 2018
nodes.paper_ref: Jacob Franek — Metcalfe's Law & Crypto Valuation (2018)

nodes.name: PMR Ratio: Price-to-Metcalfe
nodes.description: Alternative to NVT. PMR = ln(USD price) - ln(M2). Predicted 3 major BTC and 4 major ETH corrections in 2017-2018.
key_ideas: PMR uses M2 = (Active Addresses)² / Supply
PMR > 1.0: potential overvaluation
PMR < -1.25 (BTC) or -0.25 (ETH): accumulation zone
Limitations: Layer 2 growth (2026) moves transactions off-chain, weakens model
2018 assumption: 'crypto is speculative' — less true in 2026 (BTC ETF approved, ETH has real DeFi usage)
nodes.paper_ref: Jacob Franek — Metcalfe's Law & Crypto Valuation (2018)

edges.description: Fungibility concept is the foundation for ERC-20 token design
Token loss problem arises from ERC-20's transfer mechanism design
Non-fungibility concept is the foundation for ERC-721 design
ERC-721 standard enabled CryptoKitties and NFT boom
Centralization problem motivated search for alternatives, Bitcoin emerged
Ethereum generalized Bitcoin's token innovation into programmable platform
Ethereum smart contracts enable ERC-20 token implementation
Ethereum smart contracts enable ERC-721 NFT implementation
Token model enables open service governance and financing
Service governance leads to participant incentive alignment
Incentive alignment requires careful token distribution design
Open service governance is part of broader open network movement
Bitcoin's innovation evolved into tokens as fundamental primitive
Recognizing tokens as primitive leads to multidimensional tokenology framework
Tokenology framework explains diverse token use cases
Many use cases implemented via ERC-20 tokens
Ownership and access use cases often use ERC-721 NFTs
Metcalfe's Law provides foundation for NVT valuation metric
Metcalfe's Law M2 formula is core component of PMR
PMR addresses some of NVT's limitations
Ethereum network effects can be measured via Metcalfe's Law
Incentive alignment drives user growth, which Metcalfe's Law values
